county_metadata.record_losses: No known major losses. Courthouse demolished 1923 but records survived.
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Carved from Orange County Jan 29, 1849. Orange formed 1752 from Bladen, Granville, and Johnston. Pre-1849 families must be searched in Orange County records. Site of Battle of Alamance (1771, Regulator War).
county_metadata.research_tips: Strong county for deed research — ROD maintains continuous records from 1793 (pre-county, from Orange County period). Probate records from 1849 forward are well-preserved. Cross-reference Orange County for pre-1849 deeds. FamilySearch has good catalog coverage for wills and estates.
county_metadata.heritage_book: Centennial History of Alamance County, 1849-1949 (Burlington Chamber of Commerce, 1949)
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: images and indexes via eSearch system

source_profiles.source_name: Alamance County Register of Deeds Online
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deeds, mortgages, deeds of trust. Continuous from Orange County period. eSearch system with images and indexes.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Complete continuity from Orange County period.

source_profiles.source_name: Alamance County Wills and Estate Papers 1663-1978
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Estate papers, wills, probate records with images. Part of statewide estate files collection.
Will records and probate court minutes.

source_profiles.source_name: Alamance County Index to Deeds and Mortgages 1849-1941
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Index to deeds and mortgages, deeds of trust.
Court minutes, dockets, civil/criminal records from county formation.

county_metadata.record_losses: Courthouse fire 1865 (Civil War era) destroyed records. Second courthouse fire 1967 caused additional losses. Deed books survived but some gaps exist 1847-1865. Online deed portal only covers 1995-present.
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed January 15-18, 1847 from Caldwell, Iredell, and Wilkes counties. Pre-1847 records split across three parent counties. Two courthouse fires created significant record gaps.
county_metadata.research_tips: Critical burned county — researchers must use multiple sources to fill gaps. Check Caldwell, Iredell, and Wilkes counties for pre-1847 records. FamilySearch has indexes 1847-1953 that survived despite fires. Modern ROD portal only covers 1995-present. Heritage book (1986, 644 pages) is a valuable resource.
county_metadata.heritage_book: The Heritage of Alexander County, North Carolina (Sara C. Allen, ed., Alexander County Genealogical Society / Hunter Publishing, 1986)
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: indexes only for historical period; online images limited to 1995-present

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Index to deeds covering period despite 1865 fire. Microfilm.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Records 1847-1865 damaged by fire; gaps exist.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Digital scans and indexes from 1995 forward.

source_profiles.source_name: Alexander County Wills and Estate Papers 1663-1978
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Estate papers and wills with images. Part of statewide collection.
Probate court records, civil/criminal minutes from 1865 forward.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Records prior to 1865 destroyed in courthouse fire.

county_metadata.record_losses: Courthouse fire 1932 destroyed some records. However, indexed records survived — not a total loss. Deed and will systems largely intact from 1859.
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed 1859 from eastern part of Ashe County. Pre-1859 records in Ashe County. Small mountain county on Virginia border.
county_metadata.research_tips: Moderately burned county — indexed records survived the 1932 fire. ROD maintains digital PDF images from 1859 forward. For pre-1859, consult Ashe County ROD. Heritage book (1983, 555 pages, 700+ family histories) is an excellent resource. Alleghany Historical-Genealogical Society published it.
county_metadata.heritage_book: Alleghany County Heritage (Alleghany Historical-Genealogical Society / Hunter Publishing, 1983)

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed images and indexes in PDF format from county formation.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Records indexed post-1932 fire; complete from 1859.

source_profiles.source_name: Alleghany County Wills and Estate Papers 1663-1978
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Estate papers and wills with images.

source_profiles.source_name: Alleghany County Tax, Election and Road Records 1848-1913
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Tax lists, election records, road records.
Marriage licenses from county formation.

county_metadata.record_losses: 1868 courthouse fire destroyed all court records except original wills, will books, and deed books. Early marriage records (pre-1868) lost except for a few marriage bonds. Divorce records limited.
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed from western portion of Bladen County 1750. Subsequently, portions of Anson became Mecklenburg (1762), Montgomery (1779), Richmond (1779), and Union (1842). Marriage record keeping began with bonds (some from 1762-1787) but statutory registration started after 1869.
county_metadata.research_tips: Deed records are exceptionally complete from 1749 forward (pre-formation!) — the most reliable primary source. Will records survived the fire. Marriage records before 1869 are scarce; check marriage bonds, newspaper announcements, and family records. Census 1790-1950 available. DigitalNC has 9 newspaper titles (1848-1927, 3,681+ issues). Heritage book available.
county_metadata.heritage_book: Heritage of Anson County, North Carolina (1995)

source_profiles.source_name: Anson County Register of Deeds Online
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Complete deed records with grantor/grantee indexes. Earliest deed March 25, 1749. Online access 1749-1989.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Deeds preserved through 1868 fire. No known losses.

source_profiles.source_name: Anson County Wills and Probate Records 1751-1962
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Will Books (1751-1962), Cross Index to Wills (1751-1968), Estate Files, Inheritance Tax Records (1920-1962). 4 microfilm reels at NC Archives.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Original wills survived 1868 fire; will books complete.

source_profiles.source_name: Anson County Marriage Records and Bonds 1762-1979
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage bonds 1762, 1786-1787, 1762-1979. Official marriage records 1869-1962.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Pre-1868 records lost in courthouse fire except bonds. Statutory registration began 1869.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: 9 titles, 3,681+ issues. The Ansonian/Messenger & Intelligencer (1886-1927, 2,203 issues); North Carolina Argus (1848-1876, 500 issues); others.
Court dockets, civil action papers (1864-1910), court minutes.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Most pre-1868 court records destroyed in fire; post-1868 complete.

county_metadata.record_losses: 1865 courthouse fire destroyed most court records. Deed Books 15 and 16 (1799-1803) missing — possibly destroyed in Register's residence fire. Court records fragmentary with losses ca. 1814-1823.
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created November 18, 1799 from Wilkes County. Named for Samuel Ashe, Governor 1795-1798. County seat Jefferson founded 1803. Border with Tennessee and Virginia. Parent of Alleghany County (1859).
county_metadata.research_tips: Deed gap 1799-1803 (Books 15-16 missing) requires use of Wilkes County records. Court records fragmentary pre-1865; check NC Archives for copies. Marriage records began 1828 (better coverage 1853+). Birth/death registration began 1913. Heritage book in 2 volumes (1984/1988) covers family histories. Consult Ashe County Historical Society.
county_metadata.heritage_book: The Heritage of Ashe County, North Carolina (Vol I 1984, Vol II 1988; Ruth Weaver Shepherd et al.)

source_profiles.source_name: Ashe County Register of Deeds Online
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed records with grantor/grantee indexes. Includes Wilkes period records (1778-1799). FamilySearch indexes 1799-1966.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Deed Books 15 & 16 (1799-1803) missing. Otherwise complete from formation.

source_profiles.source_name: Ashe County Wills and Probate Records 1801-1968
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills (1801-1941 indexed), Probate Records (1801-1966), Cross Index to Wills (1816-1970), Estate Records (1853-1873), Executors' Records (1869-1919).
source_profiles.survival_notes: Will records relatively complete despite courthouse fire.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage records from 1828. Better coverage from 1853 forward (statutory registration).
source_profiles.survival_notes: Limited early records pre-1853.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: 4 titles, 1,147+ issues. The Skyland Post (1935-1956, 1,145 issues) is the main title.
Official registration from 1913; earlier births found in deeds, wills, censuses.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Pre-1913 entries are reconstructed/delayed registrations.

county_metadata.record_losses: No known major losses. NC's 100th and final county, formed 1911 with complete record-keeping from inception.
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: NC's 100th and final county, created February 23, 1911 from parts of Mitchell, Caldwell, and Watauga counties. Named for Col. Waightstill Avery, Revolutionary War officer and first NC Attorney General. County seat Newland, courthouse completed 1912. All records begin 1911; pre-1911 records in parent counties.
county_metadata.research_tips: All county records begin 1911 — use parent county records (Mitchell, Caldwell, Watauga) for pre-1911 deeds/wills. First federal census is 1920. Avery County Heritage Book (1984) is an essential resource. Toe Valley Genealogical Society covers this area. High elevation mountain county.
county_metadata.heritage_book: Heritage of Avery County, North Carolina (1984)

source_profiles.source_name: Avery County Register of Deeds Online
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Complete deed records from county formation. Grantor/grantee name searches, document type filtering.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Complete from 1911. No known losses.

source_profiles.source_name: Avery County Wills and Estate Files 1911-1968
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills (1911-1968), Estate Files (ca. 1916-1955), Administrators' Records (1911-1955), Record of Dowers (1913-1925), Settlements (1912-1968).
source_profiles.survival_notes: Complete from 1911; no losses.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage records from county formation.
Avery Herald (1940-1946, 254+ issues); Avery Post (1955, 1 issue).
Civil and criminal court records, guardianship proceedings, dower records.

county_metadata.record_losses: Some deed loss during Civil War (approximately 100 deeds). Will books have gaps. No courthouse fire destruction documented.
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Original formation as Pamptecough Precinct in Bath County precinct system (1705); renamed Beaufort Precinct (1712); became formal county (1739). Pitt County formed from Beaufort 1760. Pre-1760 wills filed with Secretary of State. Deed Book I (1696-1729) incorporates Bath County period records.
county_metadata.research_tips: One of NC's oldest counties — deed records date to 1696 (Bath County period). Search BOTH Beaufort records AND Bath County Secretary of State wills for pre-1760 ancestors. Deed Book I (1696-1729) is essential for earliest colonial research. Contains earliest known grant: 10 Feb 1696 to William Glover. Contact Beaufort County Genealogical Society (PO Box 1089, Washington, NC 27889).

source_profiles.source_name: Beaufort County Deed Book I (Bath County period) 1696-1729
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Land conveyances from Bath County period. Includes earliest NC grant (10 Feb 1696 to William Glover, 550 acres). Transcribed Bath County Deed Books 1-10.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Complete for period.

source_profiles.source_name: Beaufort County Deed Records Main Series 1700-present
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Real estate conveyances indexed 1696-1959; mortgages 1700-1960. Online access.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Some Civil War era losses (~100 deeds).

source_profiles.source_name: Beaufort County Wills and Estate Papers 1663-1978
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills proved in Beaufort County. Pre-1760 wills filed with Secretary of State; post-1760 filed with county. 18 Bath County wills (1702-1718) survive in Deed Book I. Abstracts 1720-1868 published.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Some will book gaps exist.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Annual tax lists showing property holders.
Marriage records 1778-1795 and 1847-1930. Gap 1795-1847 may require church records.
Court of Pleas and Quarter Sessions (1785-1868), Superior Court Minutes (1837-1960), Equity Minutes (1807-1868), court minutes from 1744.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Some gaps from Civil War period.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: 1,112 issues digitized. Local news, death notices, marriages.

county_metadata.record_losses: Courthouse fire 1868 destroyed all records EXCEPT: original wills, will books in Clerk's office, and deed books in Register's office. Major losses include court records, loose documents, and estate files from before 1868.
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed as Bertie Precinct 1722 from Chowan Precinct (Albemarle County). When Albemarle abolished 1739, became separate county. Parts combined with Chowan and Northampton to form Hertford County 1759. Known as 'Seedbed of the Colony' — critical for early colonial research. Nathaniel Batts (first permanent English settler in NC, 1655) settled in this region.
county_metadata.research_tips: HIGH VALUE colonial county. Deed books from 1723 survived the fire. Wills survived. Marriage records from 1762. ALL other court records destroyed 1868. Search Chowan County/Albemarle County records for pre-1722 ancestors. Mary Best Bell published colonial deeds (Books A-H, 1720-1757). Contact Bertie County Genealogical Society or Hope Plantation.
county_metadata.heritage_book: Bertie County, North Carolina Heritage 1722-2010 (Bertie County Heritage Book Committee)

source_profiles.source_name: Bertie County Colonial Deed Books A-H 1720-1757
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Colonial Bertie deeds published by Mary Best Bell. Earliest organized deed records for the county.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Survived 1868 fire (filed in Register's office). Complete for period.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Record of Real Estate Conveyances (1721-1948), Deeds (1723-1890), continued to present. Online search system.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Deed books survived 1868 fire. Complete run.

source_profiles.source_name: Bertie County Wills and Estate Papers 1663-1978
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Record of Estates (1728-1871). Pre-1760 wills filed with Secretary of State; post-1760 with county. Original wills survived 1868 fire. Published abstracts 1758-1799 available.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Wills and will books survived 1868 fire. Nearly complete.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage bonds 1762-1868 (abstracted); marriage register 1870-present.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Complete from 1762 forward.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Annual tax lists with property holders. Selected years available.
Windsor Herald and Bertie County Register (1833, 4 issues); Windsor Ledger (1889-1920s); Bertie Ledger-Advance (1930-present).
County Court Minutes (post-1869 surviving), Superior Court Records (1869-present).
source_profiles.survival_notes: Most court records destroyed 1868 fire. Post-1869 records intact.

county_metadata.record_losses: THREE courthouse fires: 1765, c.1800, 1893. Destroyed marriage bonds, estate records, most court minutes, many deeds. Only 1 marriage bond survives (1846). No county court minutes before 1866. Earliest re-recorded deed 1784, though some originals 1738-1779 survive at State Archives. In 1777 NC Assembly passed act to recover records hoarded by former registrar Maturin Colvill.
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Known as 'Mother of Counties' — enormous colonial territory. Cumberland carved from Bladen 1754. Anson carved from Bladen 1750. Colonial border disputes mean some Bladen residents also appear in South Carolina records. Pre-1754 Cumberland families must be searched in Bladen records.
county_metadata.research_tips: Start with FamilySearch deeds (1738-1790) and wills (1761+). For colonial era, NCLandGrants.com and Colonial Records at DocSouth are essential. Tax lists at digital.ncdcr.gov for 1763, 1781, 1784, 1788. The Byrne Account Book (PC.AB.76) at State Archives is a hidden gem — general store ledger with enslaved birth records 1762-1862. Bladen County Historical Society sells 40+ publications. For Scottish families, check Cape Fear Clans website and NC Presbyterian newspaper.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: limited — office info only, no remote searching

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Catalog #330546. Digitized microfilm. Deeds 1738-1790 and 1797-1869. Indexes 1770-1920. Colonial-era deeds (1738-1790) are the most genealogically significant.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Gap 1790-1797 due to courthouse fires. Earliest re-recorded deed 1784, but some originals from 1738-1779 survive.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Catalog #352524. Digitized microfilm. Will Index 1761-1961 and Record of Wills 1766-1892.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Pre-1761 wills destroyed in courthouse fires.

source_profiles.source_name: Bladen Precinct/County Surviving Land Warrants and Surveys 1735-1749 and Land Entries 1743-1761
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Catalog #549101. THE single most critical resource for colonial Bladen research. Earliest surviving county-level land records.
Tax lists for 1763, 1781, 1784, 1788. All digitized.
Includes Court of Pleas and Quarter Sessions Execution Docket 1761-1762 (fragmentary).
source_profiles.survival_notes: No county court minutes survive before 1866.

source_profiles.source_name: 1782 Descriptive List of the Men of Bladen County
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Revolutionary War document providing physical descriptions of Bladen County men.
Oldest Presbyterian church in Bladen (organized pre-1755). Session records from 1796.
Google News Archive: April 1919-December 1985. OldNews.com: 47,479 scans from 1929 (subscription). DigitalNC: scattered 1910 issues.
source_profiles.survival_notes: No county newspaper before 1880s.

county_metadata.record_losses: Federal troops destroyed many court records in 1865 during Civil War. Clerk's office fire in 1957 caused additional record loss. Deed records, wills (Book A 1764-1921, Book B 1908-1924, Book C 1924-1954), and some miscellaneous records survived.
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed March 9, 1764 from Bladen and New Hanover counties. Named for King George I, Duke of Brunswick and Luneburg. County seat moved multiple times: Brunswick Town, Lockwood Folly, Smithville (now Southport), Bolivia. Fort Fisher and Brunswick Town important Civil War sites.
county_metadata.research_tips: Deed books are the primary reliable source (complete 1764-1952). Wills survived Civil War despite other losses. Pre-1865 court records mostly lost. Important early settlement by Maurice Moore (1730s) and Orton Plantation (1735). For pre-1764 records check Bladen and New Hanover. Contact Brunswick County Historical Society.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Record of Deeds (1764-1952); index (1764-1963). Includes original land grants from county formation.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Complete series despite Civil War destruction of other records.

source_profiles.source_name: Brunswick County Wills and Estate Papers 1663-1978
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Will Book A (1764-1921), Book B (1908-1924), Book C (1924-1954). Probate records from 1764.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Wills survived 1865 destruction and 1957 fire.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage bonds (pre-1851), marriage certificates (post-1851). Index 1804-1974.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Complete from 1804 forward.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Brunswick Beacon (Shallotte) 1985-1994, 496 issues; State Port Pilot (Southport) 1950-1962.
Court records from 1882 onward. Divorce records 1869-1906.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Pre-1865 court records mostly destroyed. 1957 clerk's office fire caused additional losses.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Death records indexed 1914-1979.
